basics.name: Shashvat Mehta
basics.summary: High school senior interested in computer science, engineering, biology, and useful technical tools.

work.name: Technical Animation and 3D Modeling
work.summary: Created technical and creative 3D models, including animations of microscopy and laboratory equipment.

work.name: Vortex Robotics
work.summary: Coached new team members in robot design, programming, planning, and teamwork.
highlights: Taught hardware and software fundamentals for the FIRST Tech Challenge competition.
Helped lead the team to the regional championships during the 2024–2025 season.

work.name: Vortex Robotics
work.summary: Led the team as co-captain to the FIRST Tech Challenge Regional Championships.
highlights: Won FTC Inspire, Control, Connect, and Design awards.

work.name: Personal Tutor
work.summary: Tutored students in 3D modeling and coding using Blender, Python, and Scratch.
highlights: Developed a project-based learning curriculum and provided one-on-one tutoring.
Earned bonus compensation for tutoring quality.

activities.summary: Led meetings, design discussions, outreach, logistics, budgeting, and software development for a regional championship FTC team.
Paid youth coach who led weekly workshops for 20+ students on Java programming, hardware integration, debugging, and robot troubleshooting.

activities.organization: San Mateo High School Associated Student Body
activities.summary: Managed finances, budgets, deposits, reimbursements, payments, ticket sales, and records for school events.

activities.organization: San Mateo High School Mock Trial
activities.summary: Prepared testimony for direct and cross-examinations while learning courtroom protocol, evidentiary rules, and case analysis.

volunteer.summary: Taught programming fundamentals to 20+ K-4 students, adapting lessons from Python to Scratch to better fit younger learners.
Supported youth programs, library operations, community outreach, and the library booth at the county fair.
Mentored a rookie FLL team by helping students debug code, improve robot consistency, and optimize autonomous routines.
Greeted patients, supported day-to-day clinic operations, and learned about healthcare delivery and medical technology.

awards.title: AP Scholar
awards.summary: Earned AP Scholar as a 10th grader after scoring 3 or higher on three AP exams, including AP Computer Science A, AP Calculus AB, and AP Physics 1.

awards.title: FIRST Tech Challenge Inspire Award
awards.summary: Earned 2nd Place Inspire Award in Grade 9 and 3rd Place Inspire Award in Grade 10, recognizing overall FTC team excellence.

awards.title: FIRST Tech Challenge Think Award
awards.summary: Recognized for clear engineering documentation, design decisions, testing, and reflection in the team's engineering notebook.

awards.title: FIRST Tech Challenge Design Award
awards.summary: Recognized for robot design quality, maintainability, simplicity, and alignment between mechanisms and match strategy.

awards.title: FIRST Tech Challenge Connect Award
awards.summary: Recognized for structured STEM community engagement, including talks, tours, mentorship, and connections with STEM professionals.

awards.title: FIRST Tech Challenge Finalist Alliance
awards.summary: Reached the playoff finals at a State/Regional FTC Championship as part of the finalist alliance.

projects.name: SCRIBE
projects.summary: Built a Python pipeline for exploring single-cell RNA sequencing cancer data, helping researchers identify biomarkers and potential therapeutic targets.
highlights: Presented the project in the high school section of the American Association for Cancer Research conference in April 2026.
Published as the open-source SCRIBE GitHub repository.

projects.name: recOrder Animation
projects.summary: Created a 3D animation in Blender for waveOrder, a Biohub Computational Microscopy Platform technology.
highlights: Explained how label-free phase and polarization imaging can visualize cellular structures without stains.
Published as supplementary material in a scientific paper.

publications.name: SCRIBE: Explainable Machine Learning for Understanding Human Tumors — Research Poster
publications.summary: Presented with Pragnya Keerthivasan in April 2026. SCRIBE ranks candidates for further investigation; this presentation did not claim clinical or wet-lab validation. [TODO: Add Zenodo poster record and DOI after upload.]
